Matson Studios · Mockup Series 01–03

The Print Room

Sixteen print treatments built from the strongest frames in the archive — magazines, posters, a newspaper, a record sleeve, a book jacket, plates and postcards. Every image is an actual frame from the portfolio; every caption uses its real capture data. Nothing here is stock.

UNDERGROWTH

Field notes from the small world

No. 07 · Summer 2026

The Ophiophagus files: an audience with the king

Also — silk economics: what a web really costs · hedgerow arachnids of the Channel Islands, field-identified

01 / 16

Undergrowth — magazine cover

Nature journal · 210 × 280

Your king cobra portrait is the most magazine-ready frame in the archive — eye contact, symmetry, and calm menace. A condensed masthead sits over the head like classic NatGeo practice, and the cover lines borrow your own scientifically-informed voice.

Pattern XXII · Ophiophagus hannah
18–200mm · 1/50 · f/6.3 · ISO 800

02 / 16

Eight Eyes — exhibition poster

A2 · Swiss grid

The jumping spider peeking over the leaf is pure narrative — a portrait of shyness at 2:1 magnification. Set small in a calm paper field, Swiss-style, so the scale joke lands: an enormous poster about something five millimetres tall.

Spine XVI · Salticidae
LAOWA 100mm 2× · 1/125 · f/11 · ISO 800

Jersey, Channel IslandsSaturday edition · 40p

The Field Press

Est. 2026 · Natural history, reported from the hedgerow

Garden census under way: counting the parish orb-weavers

Araneus diadematus numbers look strong this autumn — and the webs are getting bigger

A cross orb-weaver holds the hub of a fresh evening web. Photograph: Steven Matson

The count starts, as it always does, with a torch and a cold half-hour after dusk. Orb webs read best in raking light: the spiral shows up as a set of concentric threads, and the owner sits head-down at the hub, forelegs on the signal lines.

This season's transect of one suburban garden turned up fourteen occupied webs along thirty metres of hedge — a healthy figure, and the females are notably large going into October. A web is rebuilt most nights; the silk is eaten, recycled, and spun again in under an hour.

Identification is straightforward: the dorsal cross of pale spots gives Araneus diadematus away at any size. What the census really tracks is structure — where webs anchor, how high, and what that says about the insect traffic a garden actually carries.

03 / 16

The Field Press — broadsheet

Newspaper · front page

Your citizen-science angle deserves newsprint. The cross orb-weaver frame is lit like a studio shot but was taken in a hedge — exactly the high-low mix a nature broadsheet trades in. The copy is written the way you actually talk about spiders: behaviour first, romance never.

Plane XVIII · Araneus diadematus
LAOWA 100mm 2× · 1/125 · f/11 · ISO 800

04 / 16

Grain No. 3 — film zine cover

Zine · full bleed

The light leak across the Axel Towers is the kind of accident zines are built around — the issue theme (“Interference”) comes straight from the frame. Typewriter caps only; the red bands do all the design work.

Copenhagen Interference · 2024
35mm · Cinestill 800T

HORTUS

Three garden studies · macro plates

MATSON STUDIOS · JERSEY — SET OF THREE, 210 × 260 EACH

05 / 16

Hortus — botanical plate set

Print set · three plates

Your flower macros all share one move — a lit subject floating on a near-black garden — so they behave like a series without any retouching. Numbered plates with genus captions turn three photos into a product: the set sells together.

Structure XII · Vertical XI · Buttercup I

06 / 16

Cavallino — collector's print

Art print · editioned

The P4 badge detail already behaves like an object study — number 7 floating soft in the background, the shield pin-sharp. Framed with generous paper and an edition line, it reads as the print-store product the brand file keeps hinting at.

1967 Ferrari 330 P4 · 2023

STAGE

JERSEY RALLY · SERVICE PARK, 22:40 ESCORT MK2 · CAR 7

Shot on Kodak Colorplus 200 — pushed nothing, promised nothing

07 / 16

Night Stage — event poster

Poster · rally programme

The Escort under the floodlights has that motorsport-programme grit — moonlit, flash-burned number plate and all. A heavy condensed masthead and timetable-mono footer lean into it; the flaws of night film are the texture here.

Ford Escort Mk2 · 2024
35mm · Kodak Colorplus 200

NEROPORT

GLOOM — THE FIRST LP

180g
FIRST
PRESSING
MS-001 · NEROPORT — GLOOM · 33⅓ RPM
08 / 16

Neroport — record sleeve

12″ LP · square crop

“Gloom” was already an album title — you captioned the frame yourself. Cropped square, that brooding tower and the grain of Cinestill 800T are a finished post-punk sleeve; the type just stays out of the sky. The one aesthetic risk of the set, taken on purpose.

Gloom · Wiesbaden · 2024
35mm · Cinestill 800T

S. J. MATSONThe Quiet FieldMS

The Quiet Field

a novel

S. J. MATSON

09 / 16

The Quiet Field — book jacket

Hardback · jacket & spine

The white stallion frame reads like literary-fiction cover art — soft light, long grass, a story implied rather than told. Serif title low in the frame, spine included, publisher's mark and everything. Film grain does the work a designer would fake.

White Stallion · Jersey · 2024
35mm · Kodak Colorplus 200

Roads South

A 35mm diary · Iberia · 2022–2024

10 / 16

Roads South — travel spread

Double page · full bleed

Dusk on the carretera and last light on the Algarve villa were shot two years apart, but they share one palette — sodium orange sinking into blue. Paired across a gutter they become a chapter opener for the film diary.

Dusk · Portra 400 · 2022
Portuguese Horizon · Portra 400 · 2024

ESTEPONA — last light on the bell tower

COSTA DEL SOL — palms at seven o'clock

11 / 16

Wish You Were Here — postcard pair

Postcards · A6, white border

Sunlit Chapel and Palm Trees are both single-subject frames against clean sky — the exact grammar of the mid-century postcard. White borders, a place name, nothing else. These would sell in a rack beside the till.

Sunlit Chapel · Portra 400 · 2024
Palm Trees · Portra 400 · 2023

FOUR BOATS · ST BRELADE — ED. 1/50

12 / 16

Four Boats — gallery print

Giclée · wide matte

The quietest frame in the archive gets the quietest treatment: a small image floating in a wide matte, one caption line, an edition number. After eleven loud pieces, this is the palate cleanser — and the one most people would actually hang.

Four Boats · Sailboats at anchor
24mm · 1/3200 · f/2.8 · ISO 200

MMXXVII

Matson Studios · twelve frames, wall calendar

13 / 16

MMXXVII — wall calendar

Calendar · overview sheet

The archive maps onto a year almost by itself: film murk for the dark months, macro as the garden wakes, harbours and palms for summer, rally season and the cobra to close it out. This is the sales sheet showing all twelve months at once.

12 frames · digital + 35mm
mixed stocks · 2022–2026

14 / 16

Eight Eyes Espresso — coffee label

Packaging · 250g bag

Merch territory: your jumping spider portrait cropped into a porthole, staring out of the shelf. Macro photography works on packaging precisely because nobody else's coffee bag has a real spider with this much charisma on it.

Grid XXIV · Salticidae

A FILM BY STEVEN MATSON

South, After Sunset

MATSON STUDIOS presents · shot on KODAK PORTRA 400 · graded in camera · sound by the N-340 · with TWO HEADLIGHTS and A MOUNTAIN · no digital intermediate · 35MM

15 / 16

South, After Sunset — one-sheet

Film poster · 27 × 40

The dusk carriageway was always a film still — vanishing point, sodium light, a sky doing the colour grade for free. Laurels, a centred serif title and a compressed credits block finish the joke properly.

Dusk · Spain · 2022
35mm · Kodak Portra 400

The Salticids — gig poster

Poster · riso duotone
